data_quality_notes: Proxy source: WB SH.STA.SMSS.ZS: People using safely managed sanitation services (%). Used as proxy for wastewater treatment plant capacity, since direct WWTP capacity data from the UN-Water report was not available as a downloadable structured dataset (only PDF annual reports). Safely managed sanitation services coverage correlates strongly with WWTP infrastructure — countries with high treatment plant capacity also tend to have high safely managed sanitation coverage. Merged 369 observations across 105 countries (years 2016-2022). GDP control available for 360 observations. Note: EN.H2O.WATR.ZS (wastewater treated %) returned no data (invalid WB code). Caveats: WWTP capacity ≠ waste recovery capacity; substantial GDP confounding expected.
summary: Using WB SH.STA.SMSS.ZS: People using safely managed sanitation services (%) as proxy for wastewater treatment infrastructure. Strong positive bivariate correlation: r=0.686 (p=0.0000), rho=0.738 (p=0.0000), n=369. Partial correlation could not be computed. Best functional form: quadratic. Hypothesis expected positive direction and log-linear form. Strong correlation likely reflects shared GDP confounding with both sanitation infrastructure and waste recovery capacity.
